Precompute the DK simulation smoother gain matrix for a KFAS model

Source: R/kalman_bridge.R
dot-kalman_sim_smoother_setup.Rd

Builds the gain matrix L (n_grid × m, standardised units) such that L %*% y_anch recovers the KFS-smoothed state when y_anch is placed at the anchor positions. This relies on the linearity of the KFS smoother in y when a1 = 0: the smoother output is a linear function of the observations, so it can be precomputed column-by-column by running KFS on unit vectors (Durbin & Koopman 2002, Biometrika 89(3), §4).

Usage

.kalman_sim_smoother_setup(model)

Arguments

model

A KFAS SSModel from .build_kalman_model().

Value

A list with components:

L

[n_grid × m] gain matrix (standardised units).

pos

Integer indices of anchor positions in the grid.

x_hat

KFS posterior mean in un-standardised units (length n_grid).

phi

Scalar AR(1) coefficient.

q_sd_vec

Standardised process noise SD per grid day (length n_grid).

h_sd_vec

Standardised observation noise SD at anchor positions (length m).

resid_scale

Un-standardising factor (sqrt(gamma)).

n_grid

Number of grid days.

Details

The returned x_hat is in original (un-standardised) units to match the output of .kalman_smooth().

References

Durbin J, Koopman SJ (2002). A simple and efficient simulation smoother for state space time series analysis. Biometrika 89(3), 603–616. https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/89.3.603
